candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line, but nothing indicates it belongs specifically to the selected COGS bucket 'Goods for Resale'. It appears outside and alongside broader operating expenses, so assigning it to COGS would be unsupported.
Separate operating D&A line, but not clearly tied to the selected COGS row 'Goods for Resale'. More likely part of overhead/operating expense structure than resale cost.
Separate operating amortization line, but no indication it is part of or specifically attributable to the selected COGS row 'Goods for Resale'.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Goods for Resale'. The visible D&A rows are separate same-level operating expense lines and are not labelled as cost of sales / cost of revenues components. Conservative anti-double-counting treatment is to add no D&A separately to COGS.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit. It sits alongside, not inside, the selected XSGA bucket rows 'Personnel costs' and 'Other external costs', so it should be added separately later.
Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit. It appears outside the selected XSGA component rows and therefore is not already embedded in the chosen XSGA bucket.

This is the summary amortization row, and its amount matches the sum of the more granular component rows beneath it ('Amortization of Capitalized development', 'Amortization of Patents,Licenses and Trademarks', and 'Amortization of Customer relations'). Per summary-vs-components rule, prefer this summary row only.
Separate D&A-related adjustment line above operating profit. It appears outside the selected XSGA component rows, so including it separately avoids leaving out an explicit operating D&A adjustment.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists only of 'Personnel costs' and 'Other external costs', and the statement shows D&A as separate same-level operating lines above operating profit. Therefore D&A is not already embedded in the selected XSGA rows and should be added separately. 'Amortization of intangible assets' is chosen instead of its component amortization rows to avoid double counting.
